summary: DHH celebrates the performance improvements coming with Mac OS X Tiger, noting significant CPU and UI speed gains. He draws a comparison to the previous Jaguar-to-Panther upgrade which doubled UI performance. He highlights that Tiger delivers both new features and better performance simultaneously. The post ends with a playful jab at Windows users, asking if they've switched to Mac yet.
key_points: OS X Tiger delivers +50% CPU/threading and +25% UI performance improvements over the previous version
Apple's OS upgrades consistently deliver speed increases alongside new features, unlike the typical trade-off between features and performance
The Mac platform offers a virtuous cycle where upgrading the OS makes existing hardware feel faster
money_quotes: Part of the joy of seeing a new major release of OS X is the enviable speed increase that follows.
You get more features, newer apps, cuter eye candy, and everything is smoother.
Have you switched yet? (Pronounced with pointy finger and tall hat). Just kidding... kinda.
key_insight: Apple's OS X upgrades uniquely deliver both new features and significant performance gains simultaneously, making the Mac platform increasingly compelling.
